operating_state: Worldwide sales increased 6.5% to $85.2 billion driven by 6.8% volume growth and 0.6% price increases, partially offset by 0.9% negative currency impact. Innovative Medicine grew with strong Immunology performance from STELARA and TREMFYA plus Oncology gains from DARZALEX and ERLEADA, offset by COVID-19 vaccine decline and ZYTIGA exclusivity loss. MedTech surged with Interventional Solutions growth primarily from Abiomed acquisition and broad-based Surgery and Orthopaedics expansion.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed as cost of products sold increased as percentage of sales, driven by commodity inflation, unfavorable product mix, restructuring-related excess inventory costs, and Abiomed amortization. Research and development investment demonstrated portfolio prioritization reducing R&D as percentage of sales while intangible asset amortization increased year-over-year. Operating expenses demonstrated leveraging through selling and administrative expense management.
material_events: Completed Kenvue separation through IPO generating proceeds followed by exchange offer disposing majority ownership for treasury stock, resulting in gain while retaining minority stake. Executed share repurchase program and maintained 61st consecutive year of dividend increases. Recorded talc litigation charge and initiated restructuring programs for R&D portfolio prioritization and MedTech streamlining.
macro_exposures: Approximately 45% of sales occurred outside the U.S. creating substantial foreign exchange exposure with negative currency impact on consolidated sales. Argentina peso devaluation resulted in charge under highly inflationary accounting. Pillar Two tax legislation implementation expected to increase effective tax rate. Volume-based procurement pressures in China and Inflation Reduction Act pricing requirements create ongoing reimbursement headwinds.
forward_outlook: Management expects completion of Innovative Medicine R&D restructuring by fiscal 2024 and MedTech Orthopaedics restructuring by end of fiscal 2025 with estimated aggregate costs. STELARA biosimilar competition anticipated January 2025 with significant U.S. sales at risk. Operating cash flows and existing credit facilities expected to provide sufficient liquidity including remaining settlement payments and talc reserve establishment.
uncertainty_profile: Talc litigation matters carry reserve with pending claims creating potential for additional exposure beyond accrued amounts following bankruptcy dismissals. Patent challenges across key products including XARELTO, OPSUMIT, INVEGA SUSTENNA, ERLEADA, and SPRAVATO through generic and biosimilar applications pose revenue risk from competition. Multiple government investigations continue including DOJ and FTC inquiries into various business practices.
